synopsis: Early in the 21st century, August, the death of one man sent tremors through the entire underworld. The Boss left everything in the hands of a man in black, a.k.a. Shadow. A worldwide battle for his inheritance has begun!

Early in the 21st century, August, the death of one man sent tremors through the entire underworld. The Boss left everything in the hands of a man in black, a.k.a. Shadow. A worldwide battle for his inheritance has begun!

A Japanese arcade mahjong title where the player faces off in rounds of traditional mahjong against customizable 3D opponents. As you win hands, the game features adult-themed cosplay interactions and character changes tied to match progress.

Ace Driver 3: Final Turn is an arcade racing game by Namco where players take the wheel of high-speed competition cars. Complete laps, overtake your opponents, and aim to finish first to win. Building on the previous Ace Driver titles, this third installment enhances the graphics and overall arcade racing experience.

The Act is a cinematic 2D action game where the player controls an ordinary man caught in a series of dangerous, over-the-top situations. Using precise QTE-based gameplay and beautifully hand-drawn animation, each scene plays out like an interactive action movie. A stylish tribute to classic action films, blending humor, tension, and spectacular set pieces.

Action Deka is a motion controlled arcade game released by Konami, with fighting and shooting attacks by a controller using the player's hand. The controller is a multi-function action controller, including shooting gun action, punching action, throwing action, and driving action.

The Brave Fangs Aero Team, part of the G.H.O.S.T. squadron from Sega's ever-popular Ghost Squad, has been instructed to shut down the rebels of the skies to end a potentially demolishing nuclear inferno.

The Brave Fangs Aero Team, part of the G.H.O.S.T. squadron from Sega's ever-popular Ghost Squad, has been instructed to shut down the rebels of the skies to end a potentially demolishing nuclear inferno.
After Dark is a fantasy‑themed light‑gun arcade shooter where players battle hordes of dark creatures rising from the underworld.

In an alternate industrial Japan, the empire harnesses a sacred ore to forge mystical blades powered by human blood. Former soldiers rise in rebellion to protect their loved ones and end the empire's brutal war.

In an alternate industrial Japan, the empire harnesses a sacred ore to forge mystical blades powered by human blood. Former soldiers rise in rebellion to protect their loved ones and end the empire's brutal war.

Akuma Mortis Immortal is an arcade horror‑themed shooter in the vein of classic haunted shooters like The House of the Dead. Players face nightmarish demons and cursed creatures freed from ancient runes, battling through eerie environments with immersive visuals and escalating supernatural threats.

The year is 20XX. The encounter with extraterrestrial lifeforms has finally happened. It wasn't the visit of the alien civilization that people once dreamed of, but the encounter with a few grains of tiny seeds. The plant has become known as 'μ'. It brought great benefits to human civilization, just as it was reaching the period of extinction. It gave birth to a wide variety of new materials. Those were used for evolution of the microdevices, which have allowed birth of advanced automated AI etc. It has ascended to an indispensable resource for humankind, but before long... It turned into a disaster.

The Alien Armageddon Standard is a 1-2 player shooter arcade machine in which players help save earth from the invading extraterrestrials! Based on the popular Sci-Fi franchise, players become a part of the action to help battle invading extraterrestrials after all of Earth's major cities get wiped off the map.
Years after Aliens and the destruction of Hadley's Hope on LV-426, two Colonial Marine squadrons return to the colony to investigate life signs and exterminate any hostiles.

America's Army Arcade is an arcade first-person shooter developed by GlobalVR in partnership with the U.S. Army. Players complete a series of Army training‑style shooting challenges, ranging from basic marksmanship to advanced combat scenarios, testing accuracy, tactical decision‑making and teamwork in a fast‑paced arcade setting.

Angry Birds Arcade is an arcade redemption game based on the Angry Birds franchise. Players use a large slingshot mechanism to launch bird projectiles in an attempt to knock down pigs and destroy structures, similar to the original mobile game's mechanics. Scoring well rewards tickets or bonuses, with jackpot opportunities for perfect shots.

ANIMAL KAISER –EVOLUTION– is an arcade card battle game from Bandai Namco where players collect and choose animal cards to pit them in combat against other animals. Players build a team of cards, each representing an animal's strengths and special abilities, and then use simple controls to battle opponents.
It features characters from Aquaplus and Leaf franchises, including ToHeart 2, Utawarerumono, Tears to Tiara, and White Album, with a main + partner combat system and animated special attacks.

Dimensional distortions are threatening Japan's Kantō region. To counter them, Maidens — young girls gifted with spiritual powers and accompanied by elemental spirits known as Arcanas — are dispatched by the Holy Spirit agency to restore balance to the world.

Dimensional distortions are threatening Japan's Kantō region. To counter them, Maidens — young girls gifted with spiritual powers and accompanied by elemental spirits known as Arcanas — are dispatched by the Holy Spirit agency to restore balance to the world.

Two months after the Kantou Incident, where Angelia Avalon attempted to break through the walls between dimensions, vast dimensional rifts begin appearing across Japan. The Drexler Institute, reborn under the name of the Rosenberg Branch, seems to be responsible. The Japanese Celestial Union and several angels from the National Academy of Metaphysics are dispatched to investigate, while agents from the Rosenberg Branch are intercepted by the Aesir. Only six days remain before Japan is destroyed, and the fighters must act to save the country.

The Drexler Institute, reborn under the name of the Rosenberg Branch, seems to be responsible. The Japanese Celestial Union and several angels from the National Academy of Metaphysics are dispatched to investigate, while agents from the Rosenberg Branch are intercepted by the Aesir. Only six days remain before Japan is destroyed, and the fighters must act to save the country.

Arctic Thunder is a Midway's first true 31k racing game. It's snowmobile 'adventure racing' game full of over-the-top, adrenaline-inducing action. With exhilarating tracks and powerful sleds the gameplay offers many levels of depth for novice and seasoned players. Game Features include; - Six unique characters and sleds - Six fantasy tracks, each with distinct experiences and environmental designs - Rewarding power-ups enhance player's gameplay - Exhilarating combat game play and amazing tricks - Bone-crunching, 'agony of defeat' wipeouts and spills - Snowmobile-like cabinet with seat shaker and fan for full-environment effects - Custom snowmobile handlebar design with hand-controlled 'squeeze' acceleration and force feedback from gameplay.

In the near future, elite pilots compete in extreme racing leagues inside magnetic tunnels built for supersonic speeds. Each race pushes both reflexes and vehicle heat management to the limit. The game delivers a futuristic high-speed arcade racing experience focused on boost control, thermal mechanics, and competitive multiplayer, originally designed for linked arcade cabinets.

In modern ballparks, teams and players compete in fast‑paced baseball innings where offensive strategy and coordinated defense decide the score. Use professional player cards to build your lineup, master powerful pitches, time hits, and run the bases. The game blends rapid arcade sports action with multiplayer options and progression via the e‑AMUSEMENT network, delivering a competitive arcade experience.

Arkham Asylum's most dangerous criminals have broken out and are creating chaos all over town. Choose your favorite Batmobile and use its fearsome arsenal to make the streets of Gotham City safe again.
Battle Climax is an arcade wrestling game, where players compete against their opposition in wrestling matches that form part of a tournament.

For centuries, humanity struggled against the forces of evil, but their efforts were in vain. Just when all seemed lost, four heroes emerged and drove back the darkness, bringing peace and tranquility to the world. Many years later, however, the terrifying Deathbringer returns, threatening to plunge the world into chaos once again. Twelve heroes, each endowed with unique powers, embark on a heroic quest to save their kingdom.

Many years later, however, the terrifying Deathbringer returns, threatening to plunge the world into chaos once again. Twelve heroes, each endowed with unique powers, embark on a heroic quest to save their kingdom.

On winding Japanese roads and scenic locations, licensed sports cars compete in high‑speed races that emphasize braking precision, corner grip and line control to outpace rivals. Compared to earlier entries, this third installment expands track variety and car selection while maintaining an arcade‑oriented yet technically demanding driving experience with multiplayer support.

Battle Gear 3 Tuned and featuring seven extra cars including the Mazda RX-8 Type-S (SE3P) and the Nissan 350Z (Z33). Also, four new tracks (the B courses) including a secret one, were added, as well as an exclusive 'Takumi Mode'. The Takumi Mode gives the player a finishing time/reduced speed penalty each time the car hits a wall.

Battle Gear 4 is a driving simulator where players race street legal cars on narrow Japanese speedways. The focus of Battle Gear 4 is on manual shifting, drifting and semi-legal driving tactics. Players can race against the computer, against the clock, or head to head against other players.

Battlegear Tuned 4 is an arcade racing game focused on speed and drifting duels. The player competes against opponents on various tracks inspired by Japan, using real cars that can be modified and tuned. The game emphasizes mastering racing lines, handling high-speed turns, and improving the vehicle's performance throughout the races.

In 2030, the elite anti-terrorist unit S.C.A.R. is deployed to neutralize a criminal organization that has seized control of key civilian facilities. Players take on the roles of unnamed soldiers (Alpha One and Alpha Two), navigating urban and industrial warzones to face heavily armed militias, armored vehicles, and automated defense systems. Their mission: eliminate threats and dismantle the enemy network before a major attack is launched. Gameplay centers on an action pedal system—press to attack, release to take cover and reload—with four primary weapons tailored to different combat scenarios. The game stands out with its dynamic scoring system, rewarding precise shot combos with bonuses and real-time feedback, pushing players to aim for perfection. Fully destructible levels feature spectacular explosions and reactive environments, deepening immersion.

In forested North American settings, virtual hunters track deer and other game with light gun accuracy. Shoot male targets cleanly, score bonus points by hitting critters, and face off in head-to-head Shootout duels. Compared to earlier entries, Pro adds competitive multiplayer and more lifelike 3D animal models.

In forested North American settings, virtual hunters track deer and other game with light gun accuracy. Shoot male targets cleanly, score bonus points by hitting critters, and face off in head-to-head Shootout duels. Compared to earlier entries, Pro adds competitive multiplayer and more lifelike 3D animal models.

In diverse global habitats, virtual hunters take aim at a wide array of game animals across savannas, forests and mountains, rewarding accurate shots without realistic gore. This edition merges content from prior Open Season and Safari entries into one expansive experience with more animals, bonus stages and hunting treks than earlier games. It retains the quick-fire, light-gun arcade action characteristic of the series while broadening the overall hunting variety.

In this spin‑off of the Shining series, characters from Shining Blade and Shining Hearts come together in a shared universe. The game depicts their battles and interactions within a tournament where different forces and ideals clash.

Within a vast network of digital worlds, the Motes, guardians of balance, see their realms threatened by Lina, a corrupted force seeking to erase everything. To stop her, they summon heroes from different realities: an adventurer, a princess, an explorer, and strange creatures from worlds like Cave Story, Code of Princess, and The Binding of Isaac. Reborn as avatars of battle, these heroes are drawn into a tournament where the fate of all existence is at stake.

Within a vast network of digital worlds, the Motes, guardians of balance, see their realms threatened by Lina, a corrupted force seeking to erase everything. To stop her, they summon heroes from different realities: an adventurer, a princess, an explorer, and strange creatures from worlds like Cave Story, Code of Princess, and The Binding of Isaac. Reborn as avatars of battle, these heroes are drawn into a tournament where the fate of all existence is at stake.

In December A.D. 2199, several years after the destruction of Ikaruga, several branches of the NOL were completely wiped out by an SS-class rebel named Ragna the Bloodedge, also known as the Reaper, whose goal is to destroy the entire NOL. The NOL, hoping to stop him, offered the largest bounty ever offered for his capture. On the other hand, Ragna has in his possession a very powerful Ars Magus called the Azure Grimoire, also known as Blazblue. As a result, the whole world, whether it be the NOL, the Ikaruga Union, or other fighters, is pursuing Ragna either for the bounty on his head or for the grimoire he holds.
A crossover between 4 different universes! Play as characters from BlazBlue, Persona, Under Night In-Birth and (for the first time in a fighting game) the RWBY series!

Long before the events of BlazBlue, humanity was pushed to the brink of destruction by a creature from the darkness, the 'Black Beast.' The world was then saved by six heroes, wielders of magic. They helped humanity create 'Ars Magus,' a combination of technology and magic, to defeat the 'Black Beast.'

Central Fiction is a 2D fighting game developed by Arc System Works. It is the fourth title in the BlazBlue series, continuing the story after BlazBlue: Chrono Phantasma. The game follows the same style as its predecessors while introducing five new playable characters.

BlazBlue: Chrono Phantasma, released in Japan as BlazBlue: Chronophantasma, is a 2D fighting game developed by Arc System Works. It is the third game in the BlazBlue series, taking place after the events of BlazBlue: Continuum Shift. The game was originally scheduled to be released as an arcade game in early Q4 2012, which was later delayed until November 2012.
In 2D environments, you can pit 18 different fighters against each other in Arcade, Versus, Story, Challenge, and Legion modes. A new mode called Abyss is also available.
Within 2D settings, 18 different fighters can be pitted against each other in Arcade, Versus, Story, Challenge and Legion modes. A new mode called Abyss is also present.

Experience the most famous battles of World War II as an elite squadron commander. From the Battle of Britain to the breathtaking air combats of Pearl Harbor, including the furious combats of Midway and the Battle of Berlin. Dominate the airspace of Western Europe and the Pacific to bring your squadron (the 'Blazing Angels') back safely. Pilots start as young recruits and evolve according to their experience to become elite pilots. Fly 46 authentic aircraft from World War II including the famous P-51 Mustang, the B-17 Flying Fortress or the British Spitfire, not to mention the Luftwaffe Messerschmitt or the Japanese Zero in multiplayer mode. Intense gameplay based on teamwork.

In a world where humans harness primal animal power, fighters from rival factions clash to prove dominance and unlock ancient might. Each battle challenges players to master timing and strategy as transforming into beast form grants powerful moves and health advantages. This installment enhances the beast transformation system and combo mechanics, expanding the roster and visuals compared to earlier entries while maintaining fast, aggressive 3D fighting action with unique animal-infused combat.

The 4.0 update adds the new mode 'Scramble Battle'. Forgoing the teamwork present in other modes, Scramble Battle is a free-for-all battle which ends when any player reaches 700 NP (New Points) or 400 seconds have time elapsed. NP are obtained capturing 'Newd', found by destroying Newd Crystals, defeating opponents or picking them up from Newd Vortexes. Scramble Battles ranking increases are kept separate from other modes, instead being their own Scramble Rank. 'Special Situations' have also been added to normal play that change the flow of battle. A new ace, 'ZENA', has been added to Union Battles.

In a city overrun by mutated bugs, a lone exterminator armed with a spray can must battle through varied locations such as hospitals, restaurants, and schools, eliminating hordes of grotesque insects and unique boss enemies. With over thirty types of bugs and collectible bonuses scattered across levels, the game blends arcade action with straightforward shooting mechanics, emphasizing quick aim, level progression, and a quirky, cartoon‑style presentation.

In a series of playful arenas, players face waves of moving targets and cartoonish creatures across a variety of mini‑shooter challenges. Using a toy‑style gun, they must quickly aim and hit targets through fast and varied stages where timing and accuracy earn points. The experience centers on dynamic, light‑gun arcade action with simple yet engaging mechanics designed for quick reflex fun in an entertaining, whimsical setting.

Caladrius is a shoot'em up developed by MOSS. The artistic direction is very oriental gothic and is created by the illustrator Suzuhito Yasuda (Devil Survivor/Durarara!!). The title also proposes an advanced personalization system and an original history.

In a world where fighters from Street Fighter II, Street Fighter Alpha, Street Fighter III, Darkstalkers, and Red Earth converge, diverse combat styles clash in intense one-on-one battles. Each character retains their unique mechanics, while team matches rotate fighters each round, creating fresh strategies. This technical crossover expands the roster and fighting systems beyond what single-series titles offer.

In the world of Castlevania, Dracula and his monstrous minions rise again, threatening villages and castles. Players take on the role of vampire hunters in this shooting game where the weapon serves both as a whip and a projectile (throwing daggers for Belmont, other weapons for different hunters), fending off monsters and bosses while collecting hearts to replenish sub‑weapons, in a fast-paced arcade style focused on aiming rather than traditional platforming.

In a world torn apart by war and magic, humans, elves, and demons vie for the legendary Dark Crystal, an artifact able to bend all creatures to its will. Heroes and warriors from every race clash in a deadly tournament, driven by vengeance, honor, or the desperate will to survive.

In a world torn apart by war and magic, humans, elves, and demons vie for the legendary Dark Crystal, an artifact able to bend all creatures to its will. Heroes and warriors from every race clash in a deadly tournament, driven by vengeance, honor, or the desperate will to survive.

In a world torn apart by war and magic, humans, elves, and demons vie for the legendary Dark Crystal, an artifact able to bend all creatures to its will. Heroes and warriors from every race clash in a deadly tournament, driven by vengeance, honor, or the desperate will to survive.

In a future threatened by the mysterious energy Chaotics, fighters from all over the world clash in search of power, truth, or simply the thrill of battle. Fast-paced 2D duels combine varied attacks, combos, and techniques. The game offers a flexible system, allowing each player to adapt their style and customize strategies to secure victory.

game_name: Chaos Code - New Sign Of Catastrophe
synopsis: The mysterious “Chaotics” energy draws fighters from across the globe, each with their own motives. Fast-paced 2D duels mix attacks, combos, and unique techniques. This edition expands the original with a larger roster, new modes (missions, survival), and enhanced customization, while keeping the series' dynamic and strategic combat style.

This edition expands the original with a larger roster, new modes (missions, survival), and enhanced customization, while keeping the series' dynamic and strategic combat style.

In a chaotic maze arena, two teams — Humans and Demons — compete to collect keys and power up generators before time runs out. Humans must gather and deposit keys to unlock the final goal, while Demons chase and tag opponents to flip team roles and gain the upper hand. This arcade action game emphasizes teamwork, strategic chasing, and asymmetric multiplayer battles, with fast-paced team combat and dynamic objectives at its core.

In 'Chase H.Q. 2,' you control various vehicles, including a modern sports sedan, a police-style sports car, and a muscular striped vehicle. The goal is to reach the end of the track, damage the criminals' getaway car, and arrest them. Some opponents use clever tactics, making the game more challenging. Progress is also based on a point system. The vehicles are fictional but inspired by real-world models.
English Synopsis missing

Set in the Space Adventure Cobra universe, a fearless bounty hunter and his partner travel across galaxies in search of a powerful artifact while being hunted by the Pirate Guild. Wielding the Psychogun, players move through stages, lock onto multiple enemies, shoot, and use a foot pedal to take cover and reload, delivering a rail shooter experience inspired by Time Crisis while staying true to the spirit of the original manga.

Set in the Space Adventure Cobra universe, a fearless bounty hunter and his partner travel across galaxies in search of a powerful artifact while being hunted by the Pirate Guild. Wielding the Psychogun, players move through stages, lock onto multiple enemies, shoot, and use a foot pedal to take cover and reload, delivering a rail shooter experience inspired by Time Crisis while staying true to the spirit of the original manga.

During a winter championship, several competitors face off in varied minigames such as ski jumping, ice races, and snowball fights. Each challenge tests reflexes and coordination. The game features simple, precise controls, short fast-paced rounds, and allows one or two players to compete head-to-head in each event.

Bringing all of the run-and-gun action of the 1988 original with 21st century graphics and controls. Bill 'Mad Dog' Rizer and Lance 'Scorpion' Bean are back to business fighting aliens in the jungles of South America--but can you survive one of the hardest games ever? Collect power-ups, unlock new levels and characters, and take on challenges in this arcade blast from the past. With updated HD graphics that stay true to the classic, get ready for a whole new Contra experience!

Two weary assassins decide they've had enough and try to quit their boss's international crime outfit. The boss doesn't take it well, and they find themselves fighting through waves of former allies as they attempt to escape the Cooper Corporation building. Players work their way through eight stages using light gun aiming and shooting, cooperating to take down foes and survive each encounter in this fast‑paced, comedic arcade shooter.

Cotton Rock 'n' Roll retains the comical world of Cotton and Silk, the game features not only from the Cotton series but also the other shoot'em up such as Sanvein's 'Time' System and Psyvariar's 'Buzz' system.

In a future ruled by vast virtual networks, two factions clash for control of cyberspace: the CosmoPolice, guardians of digital order, and the Neo‑Rebels, insurgents fighting against the system. As their virtual battles unfold, the line between reality and simulation blurs, turning technological warfare into a struggle for freedom. A Japanese adaptation of Counter‑Strike, this version reimagines the classic FPS in a cyberpunk, anime‑inspired world blending tactical combat with a futuristic aesthetic.

Welcome to Crazy Ride, a unique driving experience inspired by transportation apps like Uber... but much more fun! Get behind the wheel, pick up eccentric passengers—clowns, Santa Claus, businessmen, or movie heroes—and deliver them to their destination... without losing control! Your mission? Complete as many trips as possible within a set time, while ensuring your customers' comfort and safety. Each passenger rates you at the end of the trip: the better you drive, the more virtual money you earn. Simple, effective, and completely addictive!

Across global tracks, a driver takes seat behind the wheel of a sporty car to compete against rivals or the clock. Players must manage their lines, hit checkpoints and maximise speed to score high. The focus is on speed sensation and route choice, with multiple vehicles and circuits to unlock, and local competition against another player available.
Fans of manic shooters will find what they are looking for here: hundreds of balls, different game and scoring modes, precise and colorful graphics.

In a dynamic musical world, the player must tap or hold notes that cross paths on a large touchscreen, with each crossing requiring precise timing. Combos create chains and complex patterns, rewarding perfect synchronization. Developed by Naoki Maeda, the creator of Dance Dance Revolution, the game blends challenging scoring, touchscreen gameplay, and a varied music library, providing an immersive and technical arcade rhythm experience.

The player shoots paintballs at targets and opponents across various arenas. Quick and accurate aiming is essential to score as many points as possible. The game can be played by one or two players, alternating shots and movement, in a classic arcade shooter style without real violence.

Continuing the Cruis'n tradition, the player races through exotic, colorful tracks to finish first using boosts, sharp turns, and spectacular jumps. The game emphasizes speed and thrills, with linked cabinets allowing multiplayer battles, carrying on the arcade spirit and the series' signature fast, fun racing style.

In a future where operatives known as “Cyber Divers” enter a corporate‑built virtual reality to battle for supremacy, two teams face off in fast‑paced combat. Players select from classes with unique weapons and skills, and must work with their squad to deal as much damage to the opposing team as possible within a five‑minute round. The gameplay mixes shooting, special abilities and environmental hazards in a team‑based FPS environment.

D1GP Arcade is a drift racing game that replicates the D1 Drift Championship grand prix series. It is not like many other racers, as the focus is not on the player's ranking and speed, but rather on the number of drift points a player can acquire.

In a world where the boundaries between humans and demons are collapsing, some forge forbidden pacts with demonic spirits, becoming Daemon Brides. Bound by blood, these individuals clash to defend, control, or destroy the unleashed forces in a war where the fate of the world depends on the bond between human and demon.

In a Tokyo sealed by mystical contracts, angels, demons and humans clash to shape their fate. Compared to the original version, Additional Gain rebalances characters, adds new special moves, and makes boss variants playable in competitive settings, deepening the fights. Battles unfold in 2D with flashy projectiles, fast movement and layered mechanics that reward zoning and combo mastery, all delivered with a distinctive anime art style.

In a distant future, humanity faces a renewed assault from the mechanical alien army known as Belsar, threatening the last colonies of the galaxy. Only the elite Silver Hawk pilots can resist, battling massive bio‑mechanical warships inspired by sea creatures in a desperate fight for the survival of human civilization.

You navigate dark corridors filled with monsters, aiming and shooting with a mounted gun to survive. The cabinet combines on‑rails shooting with physical effects (vibrating seats, blasts of air, and heartbeat feedback) heightening immersion and tension in every scene.

DAYTONA® USA is the holy grail of arcade racing - First launched in 1994, this brand new entry brings Sega's #1 racer screeching into the 21st century! DAYTONA® Championship USA features three brand new tracks including a recreation of the newly renovated DAYTONA International Speedway®! In addition, the original three classic courses have been remastered for the modern era. This Standard cabinet is designed to fit in more locations. Lit from top to bottom with bright LED edge lighting the game is a head-turner. The cabinet marquee features the stylish yellow Hornet car. Have you got what it takes to take the flag again.... because DAYTONA® is back!

Have you got what it takes to take the flag again.... because DAYTONA® is back!

Night racing turns the city into a blur of headlights and neon, where every straight feels like an invitation and every corner demands commitment. You throw a superbike into traffic and tight turns, trading positions in races that never really let you breathe. The route jumps between major cities, and the pressure stays the same: keep it clean, pick your moments, and don't give anyone a gap. It's an arcade motorcycle racer built for the cabinet, with linked machines supporting up to four players. An integrated camera snaps a player photo for on-screen identification, and “ghost driver” tech lets you race against recorded driving styles.

DOATEC, once corrupt and in ruins, rises again under Helena Douglas, determined to erase the sins of the past. To mark this rebirth, she launches a new tournament. Meanwhile, Kasumi, a ninja in exile, hunts her imperfect clone, Alpha-152, a volatile creation born from DOATEC's former experiments. Around them, rivals and allies clash, each seeking their own truth in a world where the line between human and weapon grows increasingly blurred.

After the last tournament, DOATEC rests under fragile peace led by Helena. In the shadows, remnants of MIST revive forbidden research. Kasumi has withdrawn, unaware the conflict is returning. Between glory, vengeance, and redemption, new and old fighters collide in an arena where human will clashes with the lure of power.

The sky turns pitch-black and the sea rises in violent swells, as if something out there is trying to force you back to shore. Once your ship pushes forward, salt spray lashes the deck and threats start breaking through the mist from every direction. Between ambushes from the water and traps waiting on an unforgiving island, you're left with one job: keep the crew alive and fight your way to the treasure. It's an on-rails light-gun shooter built for a two-player enclosed arcade cabinet, and this special edition expands the adventure with additional stages and a new ending.

Under the stage lights, it all starts with a single choice that suddenly feels permanent. One case is yours, the rest become risks you have to manage, and every reveal shifts the mood in the room. When the big values keep surviving, the banker's offer starts sounding tempting, safety now versus the chance to chase something better by pushing on. It's an arcade take on the TV game format built for quick solo play, using button controls and paying out in tickets.

In a TV-studio atmosphere, you pick your case and watch the board tighten with every reveal, as if the game is closing in on your one big decision. The banker keeps calling with offers that sound safer each round, until you have to choose between locking it in or pushing your luck. This edition leans harder into the cabinet's “showpiece” feel, with a deluxe setup designed for a seated, more theatrical presentation.

Under studio lights, you claim a case and build your luck one reveal at a time. Big values vanish in seconds, small ones sting when they refuse to leave, and the banker tries to catch you right when confidence starts to rise. This release keeps the same core structure, but it's framed around the UK-style presentation and branding.

In a world where the end-of-year bells bring no comfort, the holiday season turns into a dark omen. The skies fill with hostile creatures, magic twists the landscape, and every stage becomes a relentless trial. To stop the collapse of this realm, you must push forward through overwhelming enemy forces and survive as the pressure keeps rising. It's a horizontal bullet hell shooter built for arcade play, supporting two players at once, with dense enemy patterns and a risk-driven scoring system.

Heroes pulled from stories that were never meant to intersect end up in the same place, as if drawn in by the same spark. Kirito and Asuna (Sword Art Online) cross paths with Shana (Shakugan no Shana), Taiga Aisaka (Toradora!), Tatsuya and Miyuki Shiba (The Irregular at Magic High School), and Yukina Himeragi (Strike the Blood), each arriving with their own motives, rivalries, and pride. When their powers collide, words stop being enough and the encounter turns into a duel, until one fighter manages to set the pace. It's a 2D fighting game built for versus play, where you pair a main fighter with a support character. The roster is a crossover of series from the Dengeki Bunko label.

Heroes pulled from stories that were never meant to intersect end up in the same place, as if drawn in by the same spark. Kirito and Asuna (Sword Art Online) cross paths with Shana (Shakugan no Shana), Taiga Aisaka (Toradora!), Tatsuya and Miyuki Shiba (The Irregular at Magic High School), and Yukina Himeragi (Strike the Blood), each arriving with their own motives, rivalries, and pride. When their powers collide, words stop being enough and the encounter turns into a duel, until one fighter manages to set the pace. It's a 2D fighting game built for versus play, where you pair a main fighter with a support character. The roster is a crossover of series from the Dengeki Bunko label.

Dengeki Bunko: Fighting Climax Ignition is an enhanced version of the original game (Fighting Climax), bringing together heroes and heroines from the famous light novels published under the Dengeki Bunko (Kadokawa) label. Characters from Sword Art Online, A Certain Magical Index, The Irregular at Magic High School, Shakugan no Shana, Accel World, and Toradora! face off in a virtual world where a mysterious entity forces them to fight to prove which ideals shall prevail: power, will, or courage.

The train is waiting at the platform, doors closed, and the timetable isn't going to forgive delays. You have to pull away on cue, control speed with real precision, and read every signal ahead, all while keeping the ride smooth. As the route unfolds, the pressure builds, because one mistake can trigger penalties and a late brake can ruin what was otherwise a perfect run. It's an arcade train-driving simulation focused on throttle, braking, and exact station stops, part of a long-running Japanese series.

game_name: Dino King Battle - King Of Jurassic
synopsis: On a remote island where time feels cracked open, dinosaurs appear as if they never went extinct. To survive, you have to build your team, read the matchups, and embrace a bit of risk, because a duel can swing on one poorly judged decision. The arcade cabinet is built around physical collectible cards you can use and keep, with in-machine reading and new cards dispensed as you play. Battles are shaped by pairing a dinosaur with action or skill cards to trigger attacks and effects.

In Dirty Drivin', players engage in vehicular combat, with gear-shaped power-ups available throughout the track. These can be used to destroy enemy vehicles by pulling a skull-shaped crank. Once destroyed, enemy vehicles do not return to the race; the exceptions are the player and their rival, a single AI opponent, as well as other players in multiplayer mode. Players can also shake the steering wheel to hit opponents, and each time an enemy is destroyed, a photo of that opponent appears at the bottom of the screen along with a taunt. The points the player earns increase the number of weapons available on the 'Jerk-O-Meter.' Crates are also scattered around the track, which can boost the player's score, double bonuses, or grant the player a free race.

In this racing game you will have only one goal: prove that the hero, Lightning McQueen, is the fastest car in the world. To do this, you will roam the town of Radiator Springs to unlock mini-games and events by talking to the inhabitants, in order to make your way to ultimate glory. Race against other vehicles or take a wild ride in the desert, the choice is yours...

game_name: Do Not Fall - Run For Your Drink
synopsis: Trapped in the world of a drink vending machine, a small hero has to sprint from one realm to the next to gather ingredients and get the dispenser running again. The catch is that the ground doesn't forgive hesitation. Every step eats away at the floor beneath you, traps keep piling up, and the real danger shows up right when you think you've got it under control, one platform before safety. It's a fast platformer built around tiles that crumble after you touch them, mixing tight jumps, dashes, and multiplayer-focused modes designed for chaotic sessions.

On an endless sheet of graph paper, a small creature starts climbing with one simple goal, keep going up. Platforms scroll past in a blur, some give you a boost, others crumble at the worst possible moment, and oddball enemies show up just when you're finding your rhythm. It's all about quick corrections, clean timing, and staying calm when the screen turns into a mess of hazards. It's a score-driven arcade platformer with horizontal movement and light shooting to clear threats, designed for short sessions and ticket redemption.

The ground shakes when the most feared fighters from the Dragon Ball Z universe finally come face to face. Between Super Saiyans, alien invaders, and sworn enemies, old grudges flare up in an instant and the fights quickly turn into all-out brawls. You recognize the shouts, the stances, and that familiar build-up of tension right before an energy barrage swings the match in a heartbeat. It's a 3D arcade fighting game built for quick duels and chaotic skirmishes for up to four players, using a lever and buttons to chain strikes, guard, and unleash special attacks.

In a festival setting, a paper dragon moves forward, dragging an ever-growing chain of tiles behind it. You have to sort things out before it gets too far, and every matched pair buys you a little breathing room. It's a puzzle game that blends the feel of solitaire mahjong with the pressure of path-based tile puzzlers, matching tiles in pairs while juggling what's on screen and what's still coming down the chain.

Dark magic spreads across realms that are losing their color, turning old strongholds into traps. Clinging to your mount, you cut through unstable skies and shattered ruins with the feeling that something is hunting you at every turn. When creatures spill out of the shadows and bosses rise to block the path, the only choice is to hold your ground, aim true, and protect what's left.

Dive into a dream world of adventures! Climb on board this beautifully modelled dream machine and be transported to a new level of excitement. Choose your dream ride and raid as many treasures as you can whilst fending off attacks by strange and incredible enemies with your Dream Blasters. Successfully complete your raid and be carried off into new realms in thrilling bonus stages. Nine unique dream experiences await you in this fantastic new deluxe game from Sega. Ride a dragon, pilot a UFO, get on horseback or put on your super suits and head for the skies! Dream Raiders features an exciting mix of exhilarating experiences that everyone will enjoy. The combination of intense motion, wind, sound and visuals combine to give players an arcade experience like none other!

Inside a tightly guarded high-rise, an agent slips between floors, vanishes into elevators, and pops out in the worst possible place to catch guards off balance. You're there to grab classified documents, time door controls, and escape before you get trapped in a dead-end corridor. The closer you get to the exit, the higher the pressure climbs, because one mistake can unravel the whole run. It's a classic 2D action game made available again in arcades through NESiCAxLive, mixing movement, elevator hops, and quick firefights with a score-driven, turn-based structure.

Inside a military-controlled tower, a biological data leak turns a recovery operation into a suicide mission. Contacts go silent, corridors lock down, and floor by floor, what was supposed to be an extraction becomes a descent into a trap. Caught between mercenaries and experiments gone wrong, you have to keep moving, even when the elevator opens onto the worst possible scene. The action is paced by physical elevator doors built into the cabinet, opening and closing during play, adding spectacle while giving you a brief breather between enemy waves.

Two agents push into a facility that's gone dark, now crawling with cyborg soldiers. Alarms drown out communications, corridors lock down, and the elevator becomes the only way forward, floor by floor, never knowing what's waiting behind the doors. You have to keep the pace, stay focused, and not let panic take over, because the mission often comes down to a few critical seconds.

When a wave of attacks shakes the world, order starts to crack and factions step out of the shadows to impose their vision of what comes next. Rumors spread about a fanatical organization promising a “perfect order,” and veteran fighters are pulled back into a conflict that spills across borders. The game takes place in the same universe as Akatsuki Blitzkampf.

In a world made of bare, untextured blocks, the space around you starts behaving like a machine that's slipped out of control. Waves of cubes spawn in, shatter into smaller pieces, regroup, and return, filling the screen with debris as if reality itself is coming apart. You have to keep your line, stay calm, and trigger the right chain reaction before the swarm overwhelms you.

In the future, tracks are no longer roads but suspended rings, ramps, and glowing corridors where a single mistake can fling you off at insane speed. Pilots live for those moments when the machine barely holds the line, you skim the barrier, and a pass is decided in the final second. At these speeds, every hit matters. Compared to the console version, the handling feels noticeably different: the arcade game leans on brake-tap drifting for sharp turns, while GX relies more on quick turns and snaking. AX also introduces T-drift, a key technique for building huge speed. The cabinet can also read a GameCube Memory Card to exchange data.

In the future, pilots battle on suspended tracks at blistering speeds, where any hit can send you off-course and the smallest opening is decided in a split second. This edition is a variant made for Cycraft cabinets, with a more ride-like seat setup, featuring stronger motion and more pronounced staging than the standard machine.

In a bowling alley that feels like it could spiral into an episode at any moment, the Griffin family turns a simple match into a loud rivalry filled with one-liners and bad decisions. You pick your character, chase the perfect strike, and the tone can shift from fairly tame to much sharper depending on the cabinet settings. Between frames, video clips drop in like punchlines timed to keep the room laughing.

After the climate collapse, a forgotten tropical island—once an ecological research lab—resurfaces. Sent to retrieve secret data, mercenaries soon discover a paradise turned nightmare: nature itself has rebelled. Amidst twisted flora, hybrid creatures, and fallen humans, survival or escape will be your only choice.

The player takes the role of a street racer competing in illegal urban races across cities like Las Vegas, Rio de Janeiro, Shanghai, and Chicago. Driving customizable supercars, the player uses nitro boosts, jumps, and drifts to outperform opponents and rise to the top of the street‑racing scene.

game_name: Fast And The Furious Drift, The
synopsis: Neon lights come up and the streets turn into a proving ground where every corner matters. You have to keep the car on the edge without losing it, cut inside at the right moment, and launch out clean so you don't get boxed in. The race doesn't wait, and even a small hit can wreck what felt like a perfect run. It's an arcade racer built around drifting, supporting up to four players with linkable cabinets and a buy-in upgrade system that lets you add credits to boost your car.

The Fast and the Furious is an arcade racing game developed by Raw Thrills (2004), inspired by the film series. It features illegal street races with 17 licensed vehicles, customization options, and multiplayer support for up to four linked cabinets. Gameplay focuses on speed and nitro boosts, typical of arcade-style racing games.

The Fast and the Furious is an arcade racing game developed by Raw Thrills (2004), inspired by the film series. It features illegal street races with 17 licensed vehicles, customization options, and multiplayer support for up to four linked cabinets. Gameplay focuses on speed and nitro boosts, typical of arcade-style racing games.

In a city that hides magic behind an ordinary surface, mages summon legendary spirits to fight over a wish powerful enough to rewrite everything. Alliances don't last, secrets weigh heavy, and every duel turns into a scramble for survival where you bet pride and blood on a single opening. With Masters and their Servants locked together, the war pushes forward until the inevitable face-off. It's a one-on-one 3D fighting game built for arcades, using a four-button scheme, execution-heavy combos, and magic-based meters that fuel flashy techniques.

Humanity is on the verge of being erased, and one organization has only one option left: travel back through time to repair breaking points in history. In each era, you have to regain control against abnormal threats by summoning heroes drawn from myth and legend. It's an arcade adaptation built around third-person 3D action, with a touch screen and card selection before fights. The cabinet loads progress via an IC card and prints physical collectible cards to use later in the deck reader.

Fighters who share the same streets but not the same background get pulled into a rivalry that won't cool off. Most of the roster comes from the Street Fighter EX legacy, with a few guests like Terry Bogard (Fatal Fury), and the goal is straightforward, make your style count before the other player does. It's a fast versus fighter where you also pick a bonus “deck” that can shift how a match develops. It's made by ARIKA, a studio with longstanding ties to Capcom dating back to the Street Fighter EX era.

In a future where conflicts are settled through mechs, pilots fight to secure objectives and gain the upper hand as a team. Between rounds, you head back to the hangar to tweak your unit, adjust your loadout, and roll out with a new setup. It's a 3v3 team-based arcade shooter focused on customization and progression. Published by Square Enix, it also featured limited-time collaborations with other franchises such as Xenogears, Front Mission, and Ultraman.

Take the wheel of classic Ford vehicles across 12 tracks divided into 5 zones. Race on-road, off-road, along beaches, through remote villages, or across the Deep South — whether it's raining, snowing, or sunny, drive your car wherever you want — the choice is yours!

Frenzy Express is a fast-paced simulation game that takes advantage of the worldwide trend of 'Scooters'. The gameplay is similar to Crazy Taxi, except that instead of picking up and dropping off passengers, the player is a courier and must pick up and drop off packages. How you reach the drop-off point is entirely up to the player, but there is a time limit and every second that passes means less money at the drop-off point. To make the game more fun, there are many ramps and other objects suitable for jumping and performing aerial stunts that earn the player bonus points.

A facility drops into lockdown and the security system collapses within minutes. Down the corridors, mechanical enemies pour in by the wave, and you have to clear the area before you're overrun, pushing room by room like a recovery operation. The deeper you get, the more unpredictable the boss encounters become, until it feels like the building itself is adapting to crush you. It's an arcade light-gun shooter with off-screen reloading, destructible scenery, and weapon pickups, with violence settings that can be adjusted by the operator.

A small frog takes on a dangerous crossing between the road and the water with one goal: reach the bonus zone before your chance runs out. It's a ticket redemption game: the better you do, the more tickets the machine pays out for you to trade in for prizes. Controls are mostly large lit buttons, so it's very easy to pick up.

On urban circuits, you chase lap times run after run, adjusting your line to changing weather and the way the car handles. Progress comes through setup tweaks and tuning, and any mistake under braking costs you immediately. It's a sim-leaning arcade racer with two main approaches: an “arcade” race mode that awards points for driving skill, and a tournament-style time attack featuring ghost cars and more technical adjustments.

When reality starts to break in familiar places, strange apparitions and invasive creatures spill into everyday environments as if the world itself is being overwritten. A rapid-response team is dispatched to contain the outbreaks, pushing from area to area to clear threats before each situation escalates into a larger confrontation.

Out in space, a swarm of invaders comes surging back, and the pilot doesn't get the luxury of thinking things through. You have to hold your line, carve through each wave with precision, and keep your nerve for the point where the threat escalates into a boss fight. Built first and foremost as a ticket redemption cabinet, it can still be switched into a more traditional arcade-style amusement mode, and operators can tune how it feels through settings such as buying extra ships, credit parameters, and payout options.

Set in a light, playful style, the game runs through short mini-challenges that focus on quick reactions, accuracy, and sometimes team coordination. It's built around a ball-throwing cabinet: you throw real balls at the screen to hit target areas, trigger actions, and meet clear quota-style objectives. This version adds a dedicated 2-vs-2 versus mode.

In this arcade game, you play by throwing real balls at the screen to trigger and clear a sequence of mini-games. The rounds move quickly and focus on aiming and timing rather than memorizing complex inputs, with clear objectives and often a time limit. The concept is designed to be understood immediately and can be played solo or with multiple players depending on the mode.

Ge-Sen Love +Plus Pengo! recreates the atmosphere of Japanese arcades, celebrating classic gaming. The compilation mixes shoot 'em ups and skill-based games, featuring a modernized version of Pengo! with HD visuals and classic gameplay where players crush enemies with ice blocks in dynamic mazes.

A block-built fortress stands in your way, guarded by enemies that keep the core target protected. The goal is straightforward: break the structure apart, clear the threats defending it, and push through each area until the final confrontation. It's a two-player redemption arcade cabinet built around a physical cannon, with shots triggered by throwing balls and adjustable firepower.

In Japan, an impossible matchup becomes real: NPB stars from the 2003 season face off against an all-star roster drawn from Shinji Mizushima's baseball manga. The hook is that two worlds that should never meet are put on the same diamond, turning each game into a series of showdowns where real pros and fictional legends share the spotlight.

An unofficial counter-terror unit is deployed when a string of attacks forces rapid intervention, often with civilians and hostages caught in the middle. On the ground, the team has to secure sensitive locations, neutralize threats like bombs, and make tactical route choices that change what happens next and which events you'll face mid-mission. It's a two-player arcade rail shooter built around physical rifle controllers with a fire selector and situational effects such as night vision or a mounted flashlight, plus an action button for context moves.

An unofficial counter-terror unit is deployed when a string of attacks forces rapid intervention, often with civilians and hostages caught in the middle. On the ground, the team has to secure sensitive locations, neutralize threats like bombs, and make tactical route choices that change what happens next and which events you'll face mid-mission. It's a two-player arcade rail shooter built around physical rifle controllers with a fire selector and situational effects such as night vision or a mounted flashlight, plus an action button for context moves.

“I ain't afraid o' no ghost” When it comes to iconic licenses, Ghostbusters is one name that has long enjoyed the status of legend. The original film provided the perfect blend of comedy and horror that many other films struggled to capture. Now with modern arcade technology, you can zap those nasty ghosts and win tickets in the process! Ghostbusters Arcade is a video redemption game developed by the arcade masters at Play Mechanix (Big Buck Hunter, Terminator Salvation, Aliens Armageddon, MotoGP, etc) for ICE. Players confront ghosts on two levels which are based on events from the first two Ghostbuster films. They then have to stop the Stay Puft Marshmellow man for the chance at a big ticket bonus!

Giga Wing Generations, the third entry in the Giga Wing series, continues the vertical bullet hell formula with the Reflect Force system, allowing players to repel enemy fire and build massive score chains. The game drops narrative elements to focus on pure gameplay and scoring, featuring multiple ships with distinct attack and Reflect styles drawn from previous titles.

In a bowling alley setting, everything comes down to a string of throws where the goal is to stack strikes and avoid costly mistakes to push the score higher. Consistency matters, and a single late-frame ball can swing both the result and the payout. It's a video bowling redemption cabinet controlled with a trackball.

In a kid-friendly musical setting, the goal is to follow a song and stay on tempo to complete the performance. The better you keep time, the higher your result, and the bigger the reward at the end. It's a ticket-redemption rhythm cabinet where you pick a track and hit in time using drumsticks on a large center drum with two smaller side pads.

The only things between the army of the undead and the rest of the city are you two and your golden guns. Make sure you've got plenty of ammo... Golden Gun takes you and your partner on a perilous trek through a city filled with zombies, phantoms and countless other hideous and dangerous creatures. You're outnumbered 10,000 to one but you've got the golden guns and that just might be enough...

A classic trackball-driven arcade golf game built around quick rounds, high scores, and connected competition. Kicks off the “LIVE” era with online play/leaderboards, player profiles, and a new baseline content set (new courses plus practice/help features depending on the cabinet/version).

The 2007 edition expands the arcade golf formula with more courses and score-chasing competition. Adds 5 new courses while keeping the 2006 lineup, significantly increasing the playable course roster.

A more “spectacle” entry that leans into bold environments and accessible competitive play. 5 new courses plus updates aimed at presentation and accessibility (more casual-friendly options, more tee/pin setups depending on edition) versus 2007.

An event- and community-focused release where every shot can matter on the leaderboards. Introduces heavily promoted features like Closest-to-the-Pin LIVE and Great Shot sharing/upload, plus new courses and expanded customization/presentation options versus 2008.
A season-style arcade golf package designed for repeat play, progression, and better scoring. An annual update delivering new courses and continued gameplay/online tuning in line with 2009.
Fast to pick up but demanding at high levels, this entry is built for competitive arcade scoring. Adds new courses and continues incremental balance/presentation refinements versus 2010.
An edition that emphasizes accessibility while keeping the series' tournament-and-leaderboard DNA. New courses plus comfort/progression-oriented options (assists/settings) versus 2011.
The 2013 release doubles down on performance, stats, and connected competition. New courses and a stronger focus on performance tracking and live events (varies by region/cabinet) versus 2012.
A 2014 installment that continues to broaden course variety for both solo play and head-to-head matches. An annual refresh centered on new courses plus minor incremental improvements versus 2013.
Competitive arcade golf focused on shot-shaping, wind reads, and chasing birdies. New courses and ongoing pacing/balance tweaks versus 2014.

Built for location play, this edition delivers quick matches and sharp, score-driven competition. New courses plus platform/ergonomic updates depending on hardware configuration (controls/UI) versus 2015.
LIVE keeps building momentum with challenges, leaderboards, and annual content. New courses and continued online/user-experience refinements versus 2016.

On a globe-spanning set of courses, the player takes on the season like a competitive tour, trying to tame tricky greens and chase the lowest score possible. New locations and reshuffled layouts keep the rhythm from getting too comfortable, pushing you to adjust your shot choices instead of relying on memorized lines. It's a trackball-driven arcade golf game where ball effects and spin selection shape both flight and rollout, backed by a redesigned interface that makes height and lie easier to read. This edition introduces the “Release” spin type and uses time-released content, including courses that unlock after launch.

A local martial arts tournament has just ended, and the newly crowned winner barely has time to breathe before trouble strikes. A shady group known as the Amoebas kidnaps Pudding, a childhood friend, and what looks like a simple abduction quickly hints at something larger: a scheme that uses science to reshape the world on their terms. With Mash, once a rival and now an unlikely partner, the two set off on a hectic journey across varied battlegrounds, facing traps, bosses, and odd encounters as they close in on the truth. The story frames a bouncy party brawler built around deliberately wobbly fighters, quick recoveries, and chaotic arenas where stage hazards matter as much as punches and kicks, with rules and modes designed for up to four players.

Groove Coaster 2: Heavenly Festival is a rhythm arcade game by Taito released in 2015, where players follow a musical track, tapping and sliding in sync with the beat. Set in a vibrant, abstract visual world, it features songs from anime, games, and the doujin music scene, challenging players to improve their scores and online rankings.

GTI Club: Supermini Festa! is an arcade racing game by Konami where players drive small European cars through open urban courses filled with shortcuts and jumps. An update and successor to GTI Club 3: Race On!, it focuses on speed, freedom of route choice, and local multiplayer for up to four linked cabinets.

After decades of conflict between humans and biological beings known as Gears, the long war is nearing its end. Sol Badguy, a Gear turned Gear hunter, continues his personal battle to confront his creator, Asuka R. Kreutz (also known as That Man). As the world strives to build a new era of peace, emerging political and scientific powers threaten to disrupt the fragile balance once again.

Anno 2187. Ten years have passed since the end of the Crusades — the war between humanity and magical bio-weapons called “Gears.” The newly formed United Nations seeks to maintain peace, but rumors spread about a surviving “Gear of Justice.” Sol Badguy, once a bounty hunter and former Gear, becomes involved in a new conspiracy linked to this reborn weapon and his long-time rival, That Man.

After the defeat of the Universal Will in Guilty Gear Xrd -REVELATOR-, a fragile peace takes hold on Earth. But Sol Badguy, Ky Kiske, and their allies continue to uncover disturbing truths about the origins of the Gears and the mysterious man who created them, Asuka R. Kreutz (That Man). As new political tensions rise between nations and the Post-War Administration Bureau, old wounds reopen and a final confrontation looms.

After the defeat of the Universal Will in Guilty Gear Xrd -REVELATOR-, a fragile peace takes hold on Earth. But Sol Badguy, Ky Kiske, and their allies continue to uncover disturbing truths about the origins of the Gears and the mysterious man who created them, Asuka R. Kreutz (That Man). As new political tensions rise between nations and the Post-War Administration Bureau, old wounds reopen and a final confrontation looms.

game_name: Guilty Gear XX Accent Core Plus
synopsis: After the conclusion of the previous Holy War, the world is still divided by fear of Gears — powerful bio-weapons created by humans. A new global police organization, the Post-War Administration Bureau, emerges and begins to manipulate events from the shadows. Various fighters are drawn into conflict, seeking the truth about the mysterious Gear “Justice” and the man known as “That Man.”

Guitar Hero Arcade invites one or two players to take the stage in an interactive musical experience. Players perform rock classics using full‑size guitars, hitting notes in rhythm to score points and excite the crowd — the arcade adaptation of the iconic Guitar Hero franchise.

Point Blank X is a light gun shooter arcade game, the sequel to the classic Point Blank (GunBullet) series. It features 72 HD-remastered stages (accuracy, memory, speed, simulation) from the first three games, plus new challenges.

U.C. 0079. War breaks out, and instead of watching history from afar, you witness it from inside the cockpit. The Earth Federation Forces and Zeon clash across battlefields where every sortie matters, and victory is less about lone aces than about squads that can hold ground, cover a push, and strike at the right moment. In the noise and pressure, one idea keeps you moving: trust your comrades, or get left behind. Battles are built around team tactics as you pilot a mobile suit, trade kills to drain the enemy gauge, and commit to base assaults that can swing a match.

Late in the One Year War, two Zeon Zaku pilots, Kurt Roswell and Robin Bradshaw, are sent from mission to mission as the conflict reaches its breaking point. From Jaburo to Solomon and into the chaos of A Baoa Qu, their rise puts them against deadlier and deadlier foes, ending in a clash with Amuro Ray's Gundam as their side begins to collapse. It's framed as an arcade, cover-based rail shooter with a Zaku-style mounted machine gun, duck-and-reload segments, and secondary weapons like bazooka shots, a heat hawk, and grenades. Set firmly in Mobile Suit Gundam, it tells the war from Zeon's perspective.

Conflicts from multiple Gundam eras collide, as if the Universal Century, the Cosmic Era, and other timelines were pulled into the same war. Pilots who were never meant to meet end up facing each other, each carrying their own battles and grudges, turning every match into a chain of duels and score-settling. It plays as a versus-focused 3D arena fighter, built around team battles and a roster that mixes mobile suits drawn from across different Gundam series.

Conflicts from multiple Gundam eras collide, as if the Universal Century, the Cosmic Era, and other timelines were pulled into the same war. Pilots who were never meant to meet end up facing each other, each carrying their own battles and grudges, turning every match into a chain of duels and score-settling. It plays as a versus-focused 3D arena fighter, built around team battles and a roster that mixes mobile suits drawn from across different Gundam series.

In 2115, the region once known as Japan is split into two incompatible realities. Frontier S has collapsed into chaos, while the 17th Far East Imperial City Management District survives through control and constant surveillance. When the two worlds begin to overlap, both sides launch Operation Stratos, sending fighters back to 2015 to erase the opposing faction from history, because only one future is allowed to remain. In this impossible war, some even come face to face with their own alternate selves, and the line between saving one world and destroying another starts to blur. On the gameplay side, it's a fast, arcade-focused third-person team shooter where each player wields two gun-shaped controllers with sticks and buttons.

H2Overdrive throws players into the heart of high-speed powerboat races, where turbo‑charged boats compete across explosive watercourses filled with jumps and obstacles. Players unleash their Overdrive boost to smash the competition and climb the leaderboards in this spiritual successor to Hydro Thunder.
English Synopsis missing

On the road, freedom is a look and a promise, but it is also a series of split-second choices. You pick a Harley, chase the next checkpoints before the clock runs out, and learn to read traffic, dodge mistakes, and keep your pace when the city tightens around you. It leans into an American road-trip mood of highways and detours, while staying focused on one thing: make it in time without losing control. As a game, it is a Sega arcade motorcycle driving experience built around officially licensed Harley-Davidson bikes and backed by licensed music tracks.

In a supernatural version of Japan overrun by demonic entities, Homura, a former warrior descended from the heavens, returns to earth to destroy corrupted spirits and restore divine order. The player advances through multiple stages, alternating between ranged attacks and sword strikes that can reflect enemy projectiles.
English Synopsis missing

On the planet Maguriboshi, two alien nations clash for control of a rare energy resource, Hoshinochi. Human pilots from Earth remotely control giant mechas to take part in this interstellar war. Each team must capture strategic points, defend their base, and destroy the enemy core, all in intense battles where coordination and weapon mastery make the difference. StarWing Paradox is a competitive arcade game developed by Square Enix and Sunrise, featuring mecha designs by Ippei Gyōbu and Junya Ishigaki, and character designs by Yoshiyuki Sadamoto (co-founder of Gainax).

On a highway that feels like a toybox turned into a challenge course, the goal is immediate: stay fast, stay clean, and keep your lead as the route throws new sections at you. It leans into that playful, larger-than-life racing vibe where spectacle matters as much as lap times. It's an arcade racer designed for a multi-station cabinet, available in 4-player and 6-player setups depending on the model.

game_name: House Of The Dead 4 Special, The
synopsis: Right after Episode 4, the AMS thinks the worst is over, but Kate Green realizes the outbreak was only the surface. Joined by “G”, she tracks the trail to a new facility where the experiments are back online, and the Magician's return makes it clear the real enemy never left. In the series timeline, it works as a bridge that ties the aftermath straight back to the core bioweapon conspiracy. Gameplay-wise, it's a co-op on-rails light-gun shooter. The “Special” cabinet turns it into a ride with two opposing screens, a motorized seat that pivots, and physical effects like vibration and air blasts.

game_name: House Of The Dead 4 Special, The
synopsis: Right after Episode 4, the AMS thinks the worst is over, but Kate Green realizes the outbreak was only the surface. Joined by “G”, she tracks the trail to a new facility where the experiments are back online, and the Magician's return makes it clear the real enemy never left. In the series timeline, it works as a bridge that ties the aftermath straight back to the core bioweapon conspiracy. Gameplay-wise, it's a co-op on-rails light-gun shooter. The “Special” cabinet turns it into a ride with two opposing screens, a motorized seat that pivots, and physical effects like vibration and air blasts.

The House of the Dead 4 is a light gun arcade game with a horror theme and the fourth installment of the House of the Dead series of video games, developed by Sega.The House of the Dead 4 continues the story of the first two games and fills in the gap in the storyline between The House of the Dead 2 and The House of the Dead III, whilst introducing several new gameplay concepts. Players can play as either James Taylor, from The House of the Dead 2, or Kate Green, a new character to the series.

The House of the Dead 4 is a light gun arcade game with a horror theme and the fourth installment of the House of the Dead series of video games, developed by Sega.The House of the Dead 4 continues the story of the first two games and fills in the gap in the storyline between The House of the Dead 2 and The House of the Dead III, whilst introducing several new gameplay concepts. Players can play as either James Taylor, from The House of the Dead 2, or Kate Green, a new character to the series.

game_name: House of the Dead Scarlet Dawn ,The
synopsis: Set several years after the events of The House of the Dead 4, the game begins during a reception at a mansion where a bioterrorist attack triggers an outbreak of mutants and the undead. AMS agents Ryan Taylor (brother of James Taylor from HOTD2 and 3) and Kate Green (returning from HOTD4) must rescue survivors and stop the spread of the “Scarlet Dawn” project, a new experiment connected to Dr. Curien's original research.

A wild race kicks off far from the asphalt, cutting through mud, snow, and battered tracks where the scenery is meant to be smashed through as much as it is to be driven across. It's not about clean lines, but about keeping momentum, spotting the time-saving routes, and using the terrain to stay in the fight. It's an arcade racing game with destructible obstacles that help fill a boost meter. On certain cabinet setups, the experience leans hard on immersion thanks to a large motion system, and the original release can be played by two people on the same machine via a “Driver Change System.”

Behind the wheel of an off-road SUV, the goal is to push through mud, snow, and broken tracks by keeping your speed up, reading the terrain, and embracing a rough-and-ready racing style. The game encourages you to smash through obstacles and save a burst of acceleration for the moment it really counts.

A string of sports events plays out like a fast-paced meet where every discipline becomes a fresh nerve test. The goal is to keep your rhythm, hit the key moments cleanly, and score what you need to stay in the running, because there's no room for sloppy timing. It's a 3D remake of a classic Konami title, built around quick, timing-focused mini events, with selectable disciplines such as ping-pong, hurdles, and long jump.

game_name: Hyper Street Fighter 2 - The Anniversary Edit
synopsis: The legendary Street Fighter II tournament returns in a special edition that brings together all versions and fighters from the original saga. This commemorative release allows players to face each character as they appeared in each classic era of the game.

In a ruined world dominated by the enemy nation Horai, a lone pilot named Shinra rises in rebellion aboard the legendary vessel Ikaruga, gifted with the power of polarity. Guided by faith and sacrifice, he takes flight against overwhelming odds to bring redemption to a dying land.

A knight takes up an ancestral blade and steps through the gates of a fortified kingdom to challenge those who would reduce everything to ash. The deeper he pushes, the more the duels feel personal, as if every victory has to be earned, until he stands before a ruler determined to crush anyone who dares defy him. The action centers on touch-driven, face-to-face duels, where you strike and parry with your hands on a large screen. It's an arcade adaptation of a game that first launched on iOS.

With the beautiful graphics created by SEGA's newest board, LINDBERGH, realism and production value have been greatly enhanced! Play Legend of the Streets, Time Attack and In-store Battle with intensity that you've never felt before! Experience the new age of racing on Initial D4! Aim to be the best of the best just like member of Project D! Customize 'Engine, Drive train', 'Intake/Exhaust, Cooling System', 'Suspension & Wheels', 'Body' and 'Electronic Components' by using the points you earned from racing. Go up into the mountains in the classic 'Legend of the Streets' mode and race one-on-one with rivals from the original manga. Rivals are drawn in watercolor with real-time 3D shading for you to enjoy the more-than-ever realistic world of Initial D. Of course, other two modes 'In-store Battle' and 'Time Attack' are also geared up to your challenge!

Initial D Arcade Stage 5 is the second game in the series released on the Sega Lindbergh hardware. A few notable differences include massive physics changes. Compared to Arcade Stage 4/Extreme Stage, which used 'drifting' physics, Arcade Stage 5 feature more realistic driving physics, which included tire and brake wear down. Although the tire and brake wear are not displayed anywhere in the game, experienced players can notice the difference in the change in physics as they progress in races. New characters that appeared for the first time in the game are Kobayakawa, Satoshi Omiya, Imposter Project D, Kai Kogashiwa (R.T. Katagiri S.V.), and Hideo Minagawa. New courses such as Happogahara, which used from Arcade Stage Ver.1 to Arcade Stage Ver.3/Street Stage (only daytime used is night) and only Extreme Stage appeared with full daytimes between day and night and Nagao, R.T. Katagiri S.V.'s home course.

A new mode, Tag Battle, was introduced. Unlike its predecessor, it was released for the Sega RingEdge hardware for the first time. The game saw a change in its driving physics. Unlike Initial D Arcade Stage 5, a drift gauge and an updated tachometer were introduced to help inform players when they were drifting. The 'Legend of the Street' mode also introduced a 'Rolling Start' since the story mode in Initial D Special Stage. The tachometer now informed players whether or not their speed was below the gear range. Online network was extended to support regions in which earlier versions of the game was known to be popular in, finally achieving both nationwide and international matches. New cars and courses were added to the game through online updates without upgrade kits like IDAS4 1.5. The game also added a mileage system to show players their total driven mileage. Mileages could be used to redeem exclusive cars that were later available in updates.

game_name: Initial D Arcade Stage 7 AA X
synopsis: Initial D Arcade Stage 7 AAX keeps the main modes from Stage 6 A.A. and adds a new Rebirth mode. It introduces Shinji Inui and returning rivals like Akiyama and Sakamoto, plus new cars such as the Toyota Sprinter Trueno 2‑door and Toyota 86 GT. The new Complete Cars category features pre‑tuned vehicles from shops like RE Amemiya, and online play adds 2‑on‑2 Tag Battles. New routes include Tsuchisaka and Nanamagari.

Initial D Arcade Stage 8 Infinity is a 2014 Sega racing game allowing solo or team competition. It updates the “Legend of the Street” mode with rival battles and adds the Initial D Factory, where players use D Coins through Initial D.NET to tune cars or buy licenses. A new Driving Training Curriculum ranks players by skill — Second, First, or Super First Class — with bonus stages unlocked by reaching top rank in all areas.

Unlike its predecessors, this game now uses a 6-speed gear shifter (similar to the ones found in the Wangan Midnight Maximum Tune games) as opposed to the sequential ones. The character designs are taken from New Initial D the Movie with entirely new BGMs that are rock songs from the films such as Backdraft Smiths, Clutcho, The Hug Me, The Valves, and Gekkou Green. A small number of Eurobeat songs from previous games are also featured but have to be unlocked. Rain condition was removed as the New Initial D movies featured no race in such condition.

The character designs are taken from New Initial D the Movie with entirely new BGMs that are rock songs from the films such as Backdraft Smiths, Clutcho, The Hug Me, The Valves, and Gekkou Green.
