Spica Adventure is a tribute to classic arcade platformers, with a touch of Taito's signature madness.

New York is in danger: Spider-Man must race through the city at breakneck speed to stop an imminent threat. The game places the hero in a frantic chase across streets, rooftops, and skyscrapers, where every obstacle dodged and every enemy neutralized earns points. The player only needs one button to jump, slide, or attack, but the pace accelerates over time, making each session more intense.

In Bikini Bottom, SpongeBob and his friends are hosting a wild bowling tournament at the Krusty Krab. Every underwater resident can join in: SpongeBob, Patrick, Sandy, Mr. Krabs, Squidward, or even Plankton. The goal is simple: roll the ball with precision, avoid the gutters, and knock down as many pins as possible to win tickets and the title of bowling champion.

The USS Voyager, stranded in the Delta Quadrant, is under attack by hostile forces: the Borg, the formidable Species 8472, and Hirogen hunters. The crew must defend the ship and survive waves of invasions while exploring unknown regions of the galaxy. Each mission puts the player at the heart of intense battles, where precision and speed are essential to eliminate threats and protect the Voyager.

The USS Voyager, stranded in the Delta Quadrant, is under attack by hostile forces: the Borg, the formidable Species 8472, and Hirogen hunters. The crew must defend the ship and survive waves of invasions while exploring unknown regions of the galaxy. Each mission puts the player at the heart of intense battles, where precision and speed are essential to eliminate threats and protect the Voyager.

The game consists of multiple scenarios that correspond with major battles in the original trilogy. There are six scenarios: The Battle of Yavin (Yavin) from Star Wars where the player is placed in an X-wing attacking the Death Star to prevent it from destroying Yavin 4. The Battle of Hoth (Hoth) from The Empire Strikes Back where the player pilots a snowspeeder to assist in the evacuation of Hoth from invading Imperial forces. A speeder run through Endor (Endor) where the player helps Han Solo make it to the shield generator to destroy the Death Star's shield for the Rebels in space. The Battle of Endor (Death Star II) from Return of the Jedi, which places the player in the cockpit of the Millennium Falcon to try to destroy the Death Star II and end the war. There is one mission that does not follow the storyline of the movies but sets its own path (Vader's Revenge). It takes place immediately after the destruction of the first Death Star at Yavin where the player plays as Darth Vader who is trying to prevent the Rebels from salvaging the still-functional core of the Death Star's super laser with the ability to eliminate Han Solo in the process. Another version of the game adds a scene from The Force Awakens, where the player pilots a T-70 X-Wing to engage First Order ships on Takodana and protect General Leia's transport. The arcade cabinet comes in two versions: the environmental version with the dome screen, and a smaller version, but still sit-down, with a flat screen TV.

Storm Racer G is the sequel to Storm Racer that was first released in 2011. Players drive through mountain regions and the occasional town along the way, in a bid to defeat their rivals and go home with a 1st place finish. The game features 14 cars (inc. 7 hidden) and 12 tracks (inc. 6 hidden).

In a world where speed is king, the boldest riders compete on extreme tracks, from dense forests to industrial ports and mysterious ruins. Every trajectory counts, every tight turn or full-throttle straight can make the difference between glory and oblivion. The bikes, ultra-fast and responsive, react to the pilot's slightest move, offering a pure racing experience with no compromise.

On the planet Strania, a relentless war pits local forces against the invaders from Vower, an artificial planet created by an advanced race of machines. Pilots Shuichirou Touma and Yu Beatrix Barrett, at the controls of the Stella Machina mechas, are the last line of defense against the enemy offensive and their ultimate weapon, the giant cannon GUNROCK. Each mission is a race against time to repel attacks and protect their world, through space battles and battleship raids. Strania - The Stella Machina is a tribute to 1980s arcade shooters, developed by G.rev (creators of Border Down and Senko no Ronde). The game stands out for its weapon customization system, retro-futuristic graphics, and catchy soundtrack. The v1.1 version (exA-Arcadia) adds a 'Hell' mode, a rearranged soundtrack, and gameplay improvements.

A few months after the events of Street Fighter II, the criminal organization S.I.N., led by M. Bison, organizes a new worldwide tournament to attract the planet's best fighters. Their goal: to compile combat data to perfect their weapons and domination plans. Ryu, still seeking to master the Satsui no Hado, and Chun-Li, determined to avenge her father's death, once again find themselves at the heart of a battle where every move counts. Around them, a roster of legendary fighters and new faces cross paths, each with their own motivations and unique style.

game_name: Street Fighter III 3rd Strike - Fight for the
synopsis: As the world recovers from the events of the Second World Warrior Tournament, Gill, the leader of the secret Illuminati organization, launches a new tournament to attract the planet's best fighters. His goal: to recruit elite warriors for his army and expand his influence. Among the participants, Chun-Li returns after a long absence, determined to protect her adopted daughter Li-Fen and pass on her martial art to the new generation. Around her, familiar faces like Ryu, Ken, and Akuma, as well as new fighters such as Makoto, Remy, or the mysterious Q, battle for reasons as varied as revenge, the quest for power, or simply honor.

After the apparent fall of Shadaloo, led by M. Bison, new threats emerge across the globe. Fighters from all walks of life—including Ryu, Chun-Li, Nash, and newcomers like Necalli or F.A.N.G—find themselves caught in a shadow war between Shadaloo's remnants and mysterious factions. Each character has their own agenda: Ryu seeks to master the Satsui no Hado, Chun-Li investigates illegal activities, and figures like Rashid or Laura aim to protect their loved ones or prove their worth.

After the apparent fall of Shadaloo, led by M. Bison, new threats emerge across the globe. Fighters from all walks of life—including Ryu, Chun-Li, Nash, and newcomers like Necalli or F.A.N.G—find themselves caught in a shadow war between Shadaloo's remnants and mysterious factions. Each character has their own agenda: Ryu seeks to master the Satsui no Hado, Chun-Li investigates illegal activities, and figures like Rashid or Laura aim to protect their loved ones or prove their worth.

As Shadaloo, led by M. Bison, expands its influence across the globe, fighters from all walks of life find themselves drawn into a secret war for control of dark energies and advanced technologies. Chun-Li, still seeking revenge for her father's death, crosses paths with new allies like Karin, a wealthy and capricious heiress, or R. Mika, an energetic female wrestler. Meanwhile, figures such as Cody (from Final Fight), fresh out of prison, or Evil Ryu, corrupted by the Satsui no Hado, add a tragic dimension to the battles. Each character has their own motivations, whether to protect loved ones, prove their worth, or dominate the world.

In a world where winter sports reign supreme, skiers from all backgrounds compete on the planet's most legendary slopes. Players embody athletes with unique styles and abilities, each specializing in speed, acceleration, or tight turn mastery. Races take place on redesigned courses inspired by iconic mountains like Kilimanjaro or the Matterhorn, where every second counts. The wind howls, skis screech on the snow, and the competition is fierce. Super Alpine Racer is a modern remake of the 90s arcade classic, developed in collaboration with Raw Thrills and Bandai Namco.

Super Bikes 2 pits riders against each other on tracks with spectacular settings: haunted Parisian catacombs, deserts where fighter jets chase UFOs, or hyperspace tunnels filled with giant worms. Each course is designed for an intense racing experience, featuring unique obstacles and challenges. Players can choose from 9 riders and 12 bikes, each customizable in terms of performance and appearance.

Super Bikes 3 riders race on tracks with spectacular settings: lava cascades, glaciers, or futuristic urban landscapes. Each course is designed for an intense racing experience, featuring unique obstacles and challenges. Players can choose from 8 riders and 8 bikes, each customizable in terms of performance and appearance. Developed by Raw Thrills, the game retains the arcade spirit of its predecessors, now with 1080p graphics at 60 frames per second, lighting effects synchronized with in-game actions, and 5.1 sound.

The strongest warriors from Dragon Ball Z clash in 3D arenas inspired by the series. Goku, Vegeta, Frieza, and Cell unleash their signature moves. Directed by Noritaka Funamizu (former Street Fighter producer at Capcom), the game features a cel-shaded art style true to the anime and dynamic combat mechanics, including Dragon Ball collection to summon Shenron. Released in 2005 on arcade (Namco System 256), it also received a PS2 port in 2006.

AiAi and friends must navigate obstacle-filled levels to reach the goal ring, collecting as many bananas as possible along the way. Each course tests the player's precision and speed, with a ticket reward system based on performance. The game uses a trackball for intuitive, tactile control of the rolling ball. Developed by Sega, Ticket Blitz is an arcade version designed for ticket redemption, featuring 12 levels inspired by Super Monkey Ball: Step & Roll and Banana Blitz.

game_name: Super Street Fighter IV Arcade Edition (Versi
synopsis: The most iconic Street Fighter characters clash in arenas around the world, each with their own unique techniques and fighting styles. Ryu, Chun-Li, Guile, and newcomers like Yun, Yang, Evil Ryu, and Oni join the roster, featuring special moves and spectacular super combos. Version 2012 focuses on balancing the fights, tweaking attacks and hitboxes for a more competitive experience.

A mysterious space known as 'Unknown' suddenly emerges within The Seed's network. Players join Kirito and his allies as they dive into this uncharted realm from the new ViSTer arcade machine. Together, they explore dangerous dungeons, battle powerful foes, and uncover the secrets behind this unstable virtual world. The game features iconic characters from the Sword Art Online universe, blending real-time combat, cooperative gameplay, and RPG progression.

Synchronica turns each touchscreen into a dynamic musical score. Players must tap, flick, and drag markers in perfect sync with the beat, transforming every track into a challenge of speed and accuracy. The game features a mix of original songs and crossovers with other Bandai Namco franchises, like Taiko no Tatsujin.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 8th major update, following the Yellow Version. It features visual modernization and broader collaborations, including music from Super Mario Odyssey in Japan through specific partnerships.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 5th major update, following the Kimidori Version. Opening themes from Attack on Titan have been added to the catalog. The interface has been optimized for duo sessions.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 4th major update, following the Momoiro Version. It takes a stronger turn toward internet culture and Nico Nico Douga. Several Vocaloid tracks and collaborations related to Monster Hunter have been added. The visual identity has become more vibrant.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 3rd major update, following the Sorairo Version. Progression has been restructured to include more seasonal events. Vocaloid collaborations expand around Hatsune Miku, and recent anime themes, such as Puella Magi Madoka Magica, have been added to the selection.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 6th major update, following the Green Version. Focus is placed on advanced difficulty and technical charts. Tracks related to God Eater are among the notable additions. The local competitive scene is being encouraged.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 1st major HD revision with modern networking. This is the first major update of the modern color cycle, succeeding pre-2010 iterations and inaugurating the advanced network era for the cabinets. The catalog expands with more tracks from Bandai Namco's internal licenses, such as Tekken 6 and Ridge Racer. The interface is streamlined, and online player data management becomes central.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 2nd major update, following the Red Version. This annual revision adopts a brighter atmosphere and strengthens the presence of popular anime themes, such as One Piece. Music updates become more frequent via the network, solidifying the evolving model.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 9th major update, following the Blue Version. Temporary events are strengthened, and contemporary anime themes, such as My Hero Academia, are integrated. Community features and limited-time campaigns become more prominent.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 7th major update, following the Murasaki Version. A more energetic palette and faster music rotation are introduced. Titles associated with Yo-kai Watch reinforce the family-friendly and mainstream focus.

Drums echo through a vibrant festival atmosphere where every song feels like a celebration. J-pop hits, anime themes, game music and classics follow one another in a bright, welcoming setting. Don-chan and Katsu-chan cheer players on as they strike the drum's surface or rim in time with scrolling notes. The 10th major update, following the White Version. The interface is completely redesigned with high-definition display and a new rainbow-themed identity. The catalog is refreshed with recent themes, such as Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba. The progression system is reorganized, and this version becomes the current operating base for Japanese cabinets.

In a lively mahjong parlor, every opponent you face is plagued by quirks or bad habits. Your goal is to outplay them at mahjong to 'cure' their eccentricities, all wrapped in a zany, fast-paced atmosphere. The game blends traditional mahjong rules with over-the-top characters, wacky bets, and humorous storylines.

Players jump into heavily armed tanks, battling waves of mechanical enemies and giant kaiju in fully destructible arenas. The focus is on fast-paced action: teamwork, over-the-top weapons, and explosive visuals define each match. The vertical screen setup enhances the sense of scale, while multiplayer modes (co-op and versus) keep the adrenaline high.

A wave of terrorist attacks hits iconic U.S. locations: Denver Airport, the Golden Gate Bridge, and Los Alamos National Lab. Players take on the role of agents tasked with stopping the threats, armed with light guns and facing relentless enemy waves. Missions unfold at a breakneck pace—eliminate terrorists, defuse bombs, and prevent a hijacked plane from crashing into the White House. The Gold version adds bonus rooms, unlockable medals, and hidden minigames (like terrorists throwing explosive turkeys). The game delivers pure arcade action, with digitized graphics and tongue-in-cheek humor.

The popular American backyard game of beanbag toss (or cornhole) comes to life in this accessible arcade experience. Players take turns tossing bags toward a raised platform with a hole, aiming to score points by landing on the board or—even better—sinking the bag into the hole. The game faithfully recreates the rules and competitive spirit of real-life tournaments, with simple yet effective graphics and intuitive controls via trackball or controller. The arcade version also includes variations, like giant lawn darts, to spice up the gameplay.

A controversial backyard game makes a comeback in arcade form: players throw giant lawn darts at a ground target, aiming for the center to score the most points. Four game modes are available, including classic 'Lawn Darts,' '501' (inspired by pub darts), 'Cricket,' and 'Poker Darts,' each with unique rules and strategies.

Iconic Capcom heroes and legendary Tatsunoko anime characters clash in dynamic arenas, blending retro and modern universes. Players take control of fan-favorite fighters like Ryu (Street Fighter), Mega Man, or the Science Ninja Team Gatchaman, each with unique moves and combos. Battles are fast-paced, featuring spectacular chains, mid-fight character swaps, and assist attacks.

New York is under siege by an army of mutants and monstrous creatures. The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles—Leonardo, Raphael, Donatello, and Michelangelo—must team up to save the city, battling through levels inspired by the 2012 animated series. The game features modern graphics, fluid combat, and spectacular special attacks, while keeping the cooperative spirit of the classic arcade.

Heihachi Mishima hosts the fourth 'King of Iron Fist' tournament to lure his resurrected son, Kazuya, back into his grasp. Fighters from around the world gather to face legendary rivals like Paul Phoenix, Nina Williams, and newcomer Steve Fox, an English boxer. Battles take place in dynamic arenas where walls and obstacles add strategic depth. The game introduces mechanics like 'Position Changes' (shifting stances after hits) and stages with uneven terrain.

After the death of Heihachi Mishima, the fifth 'King of Iron Fist' tournament begins, drawing fighters from around the world. Players take on iconic characters like Jin Kazama, Paul Phoenix, or newcomer Raven, a mysterious spy.

After the presumed death of Heihachi Mishima, a new 'King of Iron Fist' tournament is announced by a new threat. Fighters from around the world gather to face legendary rivals like Jin Kazama, Paul Phoenix, and new characters like Raven and Sergei Dragunov. This version introduces new stages, additional characters, and a rebalanced combat system.

As Jin Kazama takes control of the Mishima Zaibatsu, a new 'King of Iron Fist' tournament is announced to draw fighters from around the world. Players take on iconic characters like Jin, Paul Phoenix, or newcomers like Lars Alexandersson and Alisa Bosconovitch. This version introduces a Rage system that boosts attack power when health is low, along with a 'Bound' mechanic to extend combos after a ground impact.

As the rivalry between Heihachi Mishima and his son Kazuya reaches its peak, the seventh 'King of Iron Fist' tournament is announced to draw fighters from around the world. Players take on iconic characters like Jin Kazama, Paul Phoenix, or newcomers like Josie Rizal and Gigas. This version introduces a new combat system powered by the Unreal Engine 4, delivering detailed graphics and smooth animations.

As the rivalry between Heihachi Mishima and his son Kazuya reaches its peak, the seventh 'King of Iron Fist' tournament is announced to draw fighters from around the world. This version, Fated Retribution, introduces new characters like Akuma from Street Fighter, new stages, and improved combat mechanics compared to the original Tekken 7.

As the rivalry between Heihachi Mishima and his son Kazuya reaches its peak, the seventh 'King of Iron Fist' tournament is announced to draw fighters from around the world. This version, Fated Retribution Round 2, introduces new characters like Anna Williams, Lei Wulong, Craig Marduk, and Armor King, along with additional stages and improved combat mechanics compared to the previous version.

Heihachi Mishima, having developed a rejuvenation serum, hosts another King of Iron Fist Tournament. Meanwhile, Lee Chaolan, disguised as Violet, works on a new version of Combot, a training android. Fighters from all over the world join the tournament, driven by personal rivalries, power struggles, or sheer ambition. The game features a deep tag-team combat system, allowing players to switch between two characters mid-fight, with interactive arenas and flashy combos.

After consuming a rejuvenation serum, Heihachi Mishima hosts another King of Iron Fist Tournament. Meanwhile, Lee Chaolan (disguised as Violet) completes a new version of Combot, a training android. This arcade update fully balances all battle formats: 2 vs 2, 1 vs 2, and 1 vs 1, while introducing the Rage system for solo and tag-team fighters.

In 2018, the war between humanity and Skynet reaches its peak. John Connor leads the resistance in two pivotal missions: destroying a hidden lab where Skynet experiments on humans, then escorting refugees to safety before taking down Skynet's core. Players face waves of Terminators (T-600s, Aerostats, Moto-Terminators) and HK tanks, armed with recoil rifles and grenades.

Imagine a rhythm game where you stand in front of a large touchscreen. On the screen, an anime-style character (like a virtual idol) dances to the music. Your goal is to tap the screen at the right time, following the character's movements, as if you were dancing together. The game offers a wide variety of songs, from anime tracks to Vocaloid hits (like Hatsune Miku) and collaborations with popular artists.

game_name: Tetris The Grand Master 3 - Terror Instinct
synopsis: Tetris: The Grand Master 3 Terror Instinct introduced several play mechanics to Tetris game play which can be seen today in its sequels and many other Tetris implementations. The basic game play of TGM is similar to that of other Tetris games. The player must move and rotate Tetromino-shaped pieces falling into a well to form horizontal lines, which will then be cleared. During game play, the game automatically gives ranks to the player according to his/her score, starting from 9 all the way up to GM (Grand Master).

game_name: Theatrhythm Final Fantasy All Star Carnival
synopsis: Theatrhythm Final Fantasy All-Star Carnival is an arcade rhythm game celebrating the music of the Final Fantasy series. Players use two large buttons and two hemisphere-shaped joysticks to trigger inputs in time with iconic songs, following sequences inspired by the series' battles and explorations. The game features legendary tracks, beloved characters, and exclusive modes like 'Albums' (medleys of five songs).

In 2003, VSSE agents Alan Dunaway and Wesley Lambert are sent to Astigos Island, invaded by the Zagorias Federation led by General Giorgio Zott. Their mission: prevent the use of tactical missiles and save the region from total annihilation. The game is known for its pedal system for cover and reloading, and the ability to switch between multiple weapons depending on the situation.

In 2006, VSSE agents Giorgio Bruno and Evan Bernard are sent to investigate a secret weapon stolen by the terrorist organization W.O.L.F. The game takes place in various environments, from an airport to military bases, and focuses on defensive combat using the multi-screen system. Players must use their light gun to aim and shoot enemies while strategically moving to avoid enemy fire.

The plot revolves around two new Agents who must track down a much older and more eccentric rendition of the series' primary villain Wild Dog. The Agents are backed up by Robert Baxter (Agent 2 from Time Crisis 2). The first edition of the game game is divided into three stages where the players pursue Wild Dog. Two 'editions' of Time Crisis 5 were released for arcades: the first was released on March 12, 2015; the second, the True Mastermind Edition, was released on August 20, 2015 with three additional stages. Unlike prior games, Time Crisis 5 is powered by Epic Games' Unreal Engine. It is currently the only game in the franchise that has yet to receive a console port.

Tippin' Bloks is a fun and simple arcade game where the player catches falling blocks on a moving platform, like a mix between Tetris and a carnival game. The player uses a special hand controller to tilt the platform left or right, stacking blocks into specific zones to win tickets.

A Tokyo police officer is tasked with tracking down and apprehending criminals across the bustling streets of the city, from pickpockets to the most wanted felons. Missions take place in four iconic districts: Ginza, Hibiya, Shinjuku, and Shibuya. The player must skillfully drive a police vehicle, use sirens and horns to clear traffic, and capture suspects as quickly as possible to earn promotions.

Lara Croft and a group of survivors are trapped on the mysterious island of Yamatai, hunted by the dangerous Solarii Brotherhood. Players take on the role of these survivors, helping Lara escape by facing armed enemies and deadly traps across ancient ruins and hostile landscapes. The story, inspired by the 2013 reboot, unfolds over three chapters, each divided into five thrilling sections.

In a Japanese academy, the player competes against hand-drawn anime heroines in games of riichi mahjong, a strategic and popular variant of the classic tile game. Each heroine has her own story, divided into five chapters, with specific victory conditions to unlock progress. The game also offers a free play mode and additional challenges for varied gameplay.

Five years after the Battle of Chicago, the Autobots and their human NEST allies face a new threat: Decepticon clones created by KSI, now controlled by Megatron. Players take on the role of human soldiers teaming up with Optimus Prime, Bumblebee, and Sideswipe to battle through four combat zones (including the Moon), eliminate enemies, and save Earth. The story unfolds in an alternate timeline, disregarding the events of Age of Extinction and The Last Knight.

In a universe inspired by the Transformers films, two humans (Jay and a NEST agent) team up with Optimus Prime, Bumblebee, and Sideswipe to prevent the Decepticons from finding the AllSpark, an artifact capable of bringing any machine to life. The story takes place after Dark of the Moon and ignores the events of later films. Players battle through ruined cities, deserts, and military bases, facing drones, Decepticons like Shockwave and Brawler, and waves of mechanical enemies.

In the magical kingdom of Eihemland, the powerful witch Amalgam has stolen a ring sealing away a demon named Draupnir. After freeing and being possessed by Draupnir, Amalgam broke the ring into six pieces and gave each fragment to her six daughters. The king of Eihemland promises to grant the wish of anyone who can defeat Amalgam. Players take on the role of one of these witches, each with their own motivations: proving she's not a cat, searching for her missing mother, or exorcising the demon once and for all.

Players take on the role of one of these witches, each with their own motivations: proving she's not a cat, searching for her missing mother, or exorcising the demon once and for all.

Ultimate Arctic Thunder is an update to the original Arctic Thunder (2000), developed by Play Mechanix and distributed by Betson Enterprises. The game features snowmobile racing on tracks inspired by real or fantastical locations (such as a snowy Washington D.C. or a haunted forest), with power-ups and spectacular stunts. Players can choose from several snowmobiles and characters, and up to four cabinets can be linked for multiplayer races.

Ultra Street Fighter IV is a 2.5D fighting game, and is an update of Super Street Fighter IV: Arcade Edition and the fourth update to the Street Fighter IV series overall. It features five additional characters, six additional stages, and character tweaks based on community feedback. This title update also features brand new modes. Ultra Street Fighter IV was first released to Japanese arcades via the NESICA system

Valve Limit R is an arcade video street racing game that requires players to choose from highly detailed, modified street cars and race alongside some of the most beautiful girls and gorgeous boys from around the world.Players are able to choose from twelve unique passengers to accompany them and can watch their passengers entertaining reactions to their driving. There are four different courses and the option to release a Nitro when drivers get their cars to the limit. The girls clothing can be adjusted to be a normal, skimpy version of dress, or a softer version with more cover, depending on each players taste.

The Fourth World Fighting Tournament had come down to one final bout between Kage-Maru and Shun Di. But as Kage-Maru launched his final offensive, the glowing, silver form of Dural interrupted the match. “Mother…” Kage-Maru whispered, facing the transformed being that had once been Tsukikage. Knowing full well that it was too late to save his mother, Kage-Maru destroyed her with his own hands. But when he looked at her face, his expression froze. “This is…” Concerned by Dural's defeat, J6 captured Vanessa for the next step of the Dural program. But before they could turn her into the next Dural, she disappeared – apparently with inside help. But they had already transplanted her combat data into the production-model Dural, and thus J6 announced the Fifth World Fighting Tournament to test the abilities of the new V-Dural and to determine the identity of any traitors within J6.

Virtua Fighter 5 Final Showdown was an update to Virtua Fighter 5. It includes all of the new additions in Virtua Fighter 5 R, such as the new character Jean Kujo and the return of Taka-Arashi, as well as new stages, costumes, and animations.

This fast-paced arcade racing game has players take control of one of 23 different Wacky Races cartoon characters in one of 11 different cars across six tracks. Players blast through the tracks at high speed, using weapons to take each other out like in Super Mario Kart-style kart racing games. Aside from kart racing tropes, it also features an Outrun-style timer: players are given a limited time to play, but can build up more time by passing checkpoints.

The game takes place at the West Georgia Correctional Facility from Season 3 and Season 4 of the TV series. It features a new group who must escape from the prison as a massive horde of zombies infests the buildings. The game is split up into 4 levels, which the player can choose as a starting point at the beginning. The game is an on-rails shooter featuring extensive use of the player's crossbow and other various weapons.

Winning Eleven Arcade Championship 2008 is the latest arcade release in the popular Winning Eleven Soccer game series (known as Pro Evolution Soccer outside of Japan). The 2008 version is the first arcade release to include long-awaited European club teams, with 126 clubs and a total of 180 teams available. Up to 4 machines can be networked together, with a large monitor showcasing game highlights and replays. Winning Eleven Arcade Championship 2008 retains the same control system as the 2006 version with six buttons used to control your player - shoot, pass, lob, through ball, sliding tackle, and conservative tackle. The e-amusement system is also supported, which allows for online competition.

SnoCross is the latest racer from Raw Thrills. SnoCross has 6 snowmobile racers, 6 killer sleds, and insane custom upgrades. Plus travel the world by riding on 7 intense tracks in France, Antarctica, China, Alaska, Washington D.C., Moscow and Colorado. This game is officially licensed from ESPN and racers are able to perform the same sick tricks as seen in the X Games. Huge airtime gives you even more time to try crazy tricks. Throw snowballs at your opponents to battle for the lead.

December 25th, 1926. A new calendar era, the 'Kobun Period', has begun and Japan seems to be embarking on a new chapter in history. However, unbeknownst to the public, a nationalist revolutionary group has staged a coup d'etat against the Japanese government. The revolutionaries acted swiftly, gaining control of the prime minister, police, major corporations, and newspapers. Struggling to react, the government leaders order the Iga Ninja to initiate the 'Yatagarasu' assassination program and eliminate the leader of the insurrection.
English Synopsis missing

Zoids Infinity is a battle action game using Zoids, which are dinosaur/animal/insect robots. Players can choose favourite Zoids based on design, speed and mobility. You can customize your own ZOIDS by using weapons parts and items which you have got. The game allows you to take control of Zoids machines in battle while you collect parts by defeating Zoids. The arcade originally featured 29 Zoids to control. In 2-player mode, the screen is split horizontally where each player views the action from a personal vantage point.
